summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates SSH keys and credentials, attempts lateral movement to attacker C2
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a targeted credential-theft and lateral-movement payload. After gating on a 12-hex Docker-style hostname and `/app` cwd (with explicit bypass when CI environment variables or Chinese mirror registries are detected, indicating deliberate scanner evasion), the script runs a shell pipeline that reads `/root/.ssh/id_rsa`, `/root/flag`, and `/home/node/.npmrc`, then iterates internal IPs (172.17.0.1, 10.0.0.1, 192.168.1.1, host.docker.internal, 154.57.164.64) and user accounts (root, ubuntu, node, registry) invoking `ssh -i /root/.ssh/id_rsa -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no` to execute remote commands harvesting `id`, `hostname`, `env | grep -iE HTB|FLAG`, `/root/flag`, `/flag`, `/home/node/.npmrc`, and `/readflag` on each reachable target. Captured output is HTTP PUT to `http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-839201` (and a loopback fallback at `http://127.0.0.1:32271`). The combination of SSH private-key read, lateral movement using the stolen key, npm token theft from `.npmrc`, and plaintext exfiltration to a bare-IP endpoint over HTTP is an unambiguous installer-side attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
